{{Short description|Men's association football team representing Mozambique}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Mozambique women's national football team}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name                  = Mozambique
| Badge                 = 
| Nickname              = {{lang|pt|Os Mambas}} (The Mambas)
| Association           = [[Mozambican Football Federation|Federação Moçambicana de Futebol]] (FMF)
| Confederation         = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Sub-confederation     = [[Council of Southern Africa Football Associations|COSAFA]] (Southern Africa)
| Coach                 = [[Chiquinho Conde]]
| Captain               = [[Domingues]]
| Most caps             = {{nowrap|[[Domingues]] (124)}}
| Top scorer            = {{nowrap|[[Tico-Tico]] (30)}}
| Home Stadium          = [[Estádio do Zimpeto]]
| FIFA Trigramme        = MOZ
| FIFA Rank             = {{FIFA World Rankings|MOZ}}
| FIFA max              = 66
| FIFA max date         = November 1997
| FIFA min              = 134
| FIFA min date         = July 2005, September 2006
| Elo Rank              = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Mozambique}}
| Elo max               = 85
| Elo max date          = September 1985
| Elo min               = 151
| Elo min date          = March 2003
| pattern_la1           = _blackborder
| pattern_b1            = _mozambique1921h
| pattern_ra1           = _blackborder
| pattern_sh1           = 
| leftarm1              = FF0000
| body1                 = FF0000
| rightarm1             = FF0000
| shorts1               = 000000
| socks1                = FF0000
| pattern_la2           = _blackborder
| pattern_b2            = _mozambique1921a
| pattern_ra2           = _blackborder
| pattern_so2           = 
| leftarm2              = FFFFFF
| body2                 = FFFFFF
| rightarm2             = FFFFFF
| shorts2               = FFFFFF
| socks2                = FFFFFF
| First game            = {{fb|Mozambique|1975}} 2–1 {{fb-rt|ZAM}}<br>([[Mozambique]]; 25 June 1975)
| Largest win           = {{fb|Mozambique|1975}} 6–1 {{fb-rt|LES|1966}}<br>([[Mozambique]]; 10 August 1980)<br>{{fb|Mozambique}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|SSD}}<br>([[Maputo]], [[Mozambique]]; 18 May 2014)<br>{{fb|Mozambique}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|LES}}<br>([[Maputo]], [[Mozambique]]; 2 June 2021)
| Largest loss          = {{Fb|Rhodesia}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|MOZ|1975}}<br>([[Harare|Salisbury]], [[Rhodesia]]; 19 May 1979)<br>{{Fb|ZIM}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|MOZ|1975}}<br>([[Harare|Salisbury]], [[Zimbabwe]]; 20 April 1980)
| Regional name         = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps     = 6
| Regional cup first    = [[1986 African Cup of Nations|1986]]
| Regional cup best     = Round of 16 ([[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]])
| 2ndRegional name      = [[African Nations Championship]]
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 2
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]
| 2ndRegional cup best  = Quarter-finals ([[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]])
| 3rdRegional name      = [[COSAFA Cup]]
| 3rdRegional cup apps  = 19
| 3rdRegional cup first = [[1997 COSAFA Cup|1997]]
| 3rdRegional cup best  = Runners-up ([[2008 COSAFA Cup|2008]], [[2015 COSAFA Cup|2015]])
| medaltemplates        = 
{{MedalCompetition|[[COSAFA Cup]]}}
{{MedalSilver|[[2008 COSAFA Cup|2008 South Africa]]|}}
{{MedalSilver|[[2015 COSAFA Cup|2015 South Africa]]|}}
{{MedalBronze|[[1997 COSAFA Cup|1997 Southern Africa]]|}}
{{MedalBronze|[[2009 COSAFA Cup|2009 Zimbabwe]]|}}
{{MedalBronze|[[2024 COSAFA Cup|2024 South Africa]]|}}
|pattern_b3=_mozambique1921t|pattern_ra3=_blackborder|pattern_la3=_blackborder|shorts3=fff200|leftarm3=fff200|rightarm3=fff200|socks3=fff200}}

The '''Mozambique national football team''' ({{langx|pt|Seleção Moçambicana de Futebol}}) represents [[Mozambique]] in men's international [[association football|football]] competitions and is controlled by the [[Mozambican Football Federation]], the governing body for [[football in Mozambique]]. Mozambique have never qualified for a [[FIFA World Cup]], but they have qualified for six [[Africa Cup of Nations]], having advanced to the knockout stage for the first time in [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]. They have also appeared at the [[African Nations Championship]] on two occasions where, at the [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022 edition]], they claimed their first ever victory and reached the knockout stage of an 11-aside [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] tournament for the first time history. In 1997, the Mozambique Football Federation became a founding member of COSAFA.

Mozambique's home ground is [[Estádio do Zimpeto]] in [[Zimpeto]] in the capital city [[Maputo]], and can hold 42,000 spectators. The team's current head coach is former player [[Chiquinho Conde]], who became manager in July 2019,<ref>{{Cite book |date=2021 |title=Matine takes over from Xavier as new Mozambique coach |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/49078493 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190726000628/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/49078493 |archive-date=July 26, 2019 |access-date=July 25, 2019 |via=www.bbc.co.uk |isbn=9781961443174 |last1=Adams |first1=Amanda |publisher=Amazon Digital Services LLC - Kdp }}</ref> replacing previous head coach and former Portugal international [[Abel Xavier]], who had been in charge since February 2016.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/35408438|title=Mozambique appoint Abel Xavier as new national coach|work=BBC Sport |date=January 26, 2016|access-date=February 13, 2018|archive-date=December 25, 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171225053538/http://www.bbc.com/sport/football/35408438|url-status=live}}</ref>

==History==

===Beginnings===
On the day of independence in 1975, Mozambique played its first ever match; a friendly against [[Zambia national football team|Zambia]], winning 2–1. Two years later, [[Cuba national football team|Cuba]] became Mozambique's first non-African opponent when the two countries met in Mozambique, with Cuba winning 2–0.<ref>{{cite web|title=Mozambique – List of International Matches|website=[[RSSSF]]|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/moz-intres.html|access-date=6 June 2018|archive-date=6 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221206133458/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/moz-intres.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Mozambique entered World Cup qualifying for the first time in the [[1982 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|1982 qualifying competition]]. Mozambique were defeated 7–3 over two legs by [[DR Congo national football team|Zaire]] in the first round.

===1986 Africa Cup of Nations===
Mozambique qualified for its first [[Africa Cup of Nations]] in [[1986 Africa Cup of Nations|1986]]. In the [[1986 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|qualifying]] competition they beat [[Mauritius national football team|Mauritius]], [[Malawi national football team|Malawi]] (on penalties), and finally [[Libya national football team|Libya]], winning again on penalties.

At the final tournament in [[Egypt]], Mozambique were placed in Group A along with [[Senegal national football team|Senegal]], [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]] and hosts [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]]. They lost all their games 3–0, 2–0 and 2–0, not scoring a single goal.

===1996 Africa Cup of Nations===
Mozambique had to wait 10 years to qualify for another [[Africa Cup of Nations]], as they qualified for the [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]] tournament in [[South Africa]]. They were placed in Group D along with [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]], [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]] and [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]]. Mozambique played their first game against Tunisia in [[Port Elizabeth]], drawing 1–1 with [[Tico-Tico]] scoring their first ever goal at the finals in the 4th minute. They then went on to lose 1–0 to Ivory Coast and 2–0 to Ghana, thus eliminating them from the tournament.

===1998 Africa Cup of Nations===
Two years later, Mozambique qualified for their third [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|Africa Cup of Nations]] held in [[Burkina Faso]]. They were again placed in group D along with [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]], [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]] and [[Zambia national football team|Zambia]]. Mozambique lost their first game against eventual tournament winners Egypt 2–0, both goals coming from [[Hossam Hassan]]. In their second game they again lost to Morocco 3–0, therefore eliminating them from the tournament with one game still remaining. In their last game against Zambia, they drew 1–1, their first goal of the tournament. This would prove to be their last African Cup of Nations game for 12 years.

===2010 FIFA World Cup qualification===
[[File:Mozambique-Tunisia match 2009 (1).jpg|thumb|200px|The national football team of Mozambique before a game against Tunisia]]
[[File:Armando Sá photo.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Armando Sá]] played six games for Mozambique]]

Mozambique entered the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2010 FIFA World Cup qualification]] in the second round, and were placed in Group 7 with [[Botswana national football team|Botswana]], [[Madagascar national football team|Madagascar]] and African football giants [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]]. They made a terrible start to qualifying, losing to Ivory Coast and minnows Botswana 1–0 and 2–1, and drawing 1–1 with Madagascar. Mozambique then went on to beat Madagascar 3–0 in [[Antananarivo]] with goals coming from [[Tico-Tico]], [[Carlos Chimomole|Carlitos]] and [[Domingues (Mozambican footballer)|Domingues]]. They then drew 1–1 with Ivory Coast and beat Botswana 1–0 in [[Gaborone]] to qualify for the third Round.

Mozambique were one of the lowest seeded teams in the third round, and were placed in Group B with [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]], [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]] and [[Kenya national football team|Kenya]]. They went on to draw their first game against giants Nigeria 0–0 in [[Maputo]]. They then went on to lose their next to games against Tunisia and Kenya 2–0 and 2–1, now making it a struggle to qualify for their first FIFA World Cup. In the next game, they beat Kenya 1–0 with [[Tico-Tico]] scoring, but then a loss to Nigeria eliminated them from qualifying. In the last game they beat Tunisia 1–0 in a victory that stopped Tunisia from qualifying. Despite not qualifying for the World Cup, this win was enough to secure third place and qualification for the [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations]] in [[Angola]].

===2010 Africa Cup of Nations===
After a 12-year absence from [[Africa Cup of Nations]] football, Mozambique were placed in Group C with [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]], [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]] and [[Benin national football team|Benin]]. In their first game, they played Benin, drawing 2–2 after being 2–0 down, with goals coming from [[Almiro Lobo|Miro]] and [[Carlos Fumo Gonçalves|Fumo]]. They then went on to lose 2–0 to eventual tournament winners Egypt and 3–0 to Nigeria, thus eliminating them from the tournament. After the tournament, all-time top scorer and captain [[Tico-Tico]] retired from international football.

===Recent years===
Mozambique had several close misses when it came to reaching another AFCON Finals. During the [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2013 qualifiers]], they reached the final round and beat [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]] 2–0 in the first leg in [[Maputo]]. However, they were beaten 4–0 in [[Marrakesh]] four days later. During the [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2019 qualifiers]] the ''Mambas'' were only denied by a [[Guinea-Bissau national football team|Guinea-Bissau]] equaliser in stoppage time at the end of their final [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations qualification Group K|Group K]] match.

Finally, in 2023, Mozambique qualified for the AFCON for the first time since 2010 after beating [[Benin]] 3-2 at home on the final day of qualification. They would finish bottom of their group at the finals, drawing 2-2 with both [[Egypt]] and [[Ghana]] in their first and last games respectively but losing 3-0 to [[Cape Verde]] in between.

Mozambique qualified for the [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]], which is set to be their sixth appearance at the tournament.

==Recent results and fixtures==
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 5 September
| time       = {{UTZ|19:00|3}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|UGA}}
| score      = 4–0
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020802
| team2      = {{fb|MOZ}}
| goals1     =
*[[Allan Okello|Okello]] {{goal|48}}
*[[Rogers Mato|Mato]] {{goal|70||84}}
*[[Elio Capradossi|Capradossi]] {{goal|90}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Mandela National Stadium]]
| location   = [[Kampala]], Uganda
| attendance = 
| referee    = Vincent Kaboré ([[Burkinabé Football Federation|Burkina Faso]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 8 September
| time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|MOZ}}
| score      = 2–0
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020800
| team2      = {{fb|BOT}}
| goals1     =
*[[Witi]] {{goal|6}}
*[[Faisal Bangal|Bangal]] {{goal|71}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Estádio do Zimpeto]]
| location   = [[Maputo]], Mozambique
| attendance = 20,000
| referee    = [[Bamlak Tessema Weyesa]] ([[Ethiopian Football Federation|Ethiopia]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 9 October
| time       = {{UTZ|18:00|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|MOZ}}
| score      = 1–2
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021065
| team2      = {{fb|GUI}}
| goals1     =
*[[Reinildo Mandava|Mandava]] {{goal|19}}
| goals2     =
*[[Abdul Karim Traoré|Traoré]] {{goal|2||59}}
| stadium    = [[Estádio do Zimpeto]]
| location   = [[Maputo]], Mozambique
| attendance = 
| referee    = Adissa Abdul Raphiou Ligali ([[Benin Football Federation|Benin]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 14 October
| time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SOM}}
| score      = 0–1
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021069
| team2      = {{fb|MOZ}}
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
*[[Geny Catamo|Catamo]] {{goal|6}}
| stadium    = [[Miloud Hadefi Stadium]]
| location   = [[Oran]], Algeria
| attendance = 
| referee    = Ahmed Abdulrazg ([[Libyan Football Federation|Libya]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|date       = 14 November
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MAR}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|MOZ}}
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/cesdwwnxbc5fmajgroc0hqzy2/8pw8cm9fuczk7lmxm1oxjdyc4/1rj1740ys47h5s938mfswsh78/8kk1qo9wy235epjk053c4a0pg?date=2025-11-14
|goals1     = *[[Azzedine Ounahi|Ounahi]] {{goal|7}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Ibn Batouta Stadium]]
|location   = [[Tangier]], Morocco
|attendance = 60,000
|referee    = [[Moussa Diou]] ([[Football Federation of the Islamic Republic of Mauritania|Mauritania]])
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|date= 17 November 
|time= {{UTZ|16:00|1}}
|round= [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|score= 2–2
|report= https://www.soccerway.com/match/chad-t8VC3nNA/mozambique-2aBNiivt/?mid=U9s3HUkD
|team1= {{fb-rt|CHA}}
|goals1= 
*[[Marius Mouandilmadji|Mouandilmadji]] {{goal|45+2}}
*[[Mahamat Thiam|M. Thiam]] {{goal|90+1|pen.}}
|team2= {{fb|MOZ}}
|goals2= 
*[[Geny Catamo|Catamo]] {{goal|79|pen.}}
*[[Gildo Vilanculos|Gildo]] {{goal|90+6}}
|stadium= [[Berrechid Municipal Stadium|Stade Municipal De Berrechid]]
|location= [[Berrechid]], Morocco
|attendance=
|referee= 
|result= D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly match]]
|date       = 17 December
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|0}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ANG}}
|score      = 4–1 
|note        = The match consisted of two 45-minute halves plus a single, extra period of 30 minutes.
|team2      = {{fb|MOZ}}
|goals1     =
*[[Gelson Dala]] {{goal|25}}
*[[Zito Luvumbo|Luvumbo]] {{goal|81}}
*[[David Carmo|Carmo]] {{goal|90}}
*[[Rui Modesto|Modesto]] {{goal|120}}
|goals2     =
*[[Witi]] {{goal|104|pen}}
|stadium    = Parque das Cidades Training Center
|location   = [[Almancil]], Portugal
|attendance = [[Behind closed doors (sport)|Behind closed doors]]
|referee    = 
|report     = https://www.fmf.co.mz/news/details/1014
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations#Group F|2025 AFCON GS]]
|date       = 24 December
|time       = {{UTZ|18:30|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CIV}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|MOZ}}
|goals1     = [[Amad Diallo|Amad]] {{goal|49}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Marrakesh Stadium]] 
|location   = [[Marrakesh]], Morocco
|attendance = 13,041
|referee    = [[Samuel Uwikunda]] ([[Rwanda Football Federation|Rwanda]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/400132623/1z35p4iuhfxxdfaqjwzkqn2fo/207mir3y73ql1144i13i8xe6s/bue4tjgpmwuzeh0gkuehbnaxg
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations#Group F|2025 AFCON GS]]
|date       = 28 December
|time       = {{UTZ|13:30|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GAB}}
|score      = 2–3
|team2      = {{fb|MOZ}}
|goals1     = *[[Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang|Aubameyang]] {{goal|45+5}}
*[[Alex Moucketou-Moussounda|Moussounda]] {{goal|76}}
|goals2     = *[[Faisal Bangal|Bangal]] {{goal|37}}
*[[Geny Catamo|Catamo]] {{goal|42|pen}}
*[[Diogo Calila|Calila]] {{goal|52}}
|stadium    = [[Adrar Stadium]]
|location   = [[Agadir]], Morocco
|attendance = 9,796
|referee    = Mehrez Melki ([[Tunisian Football Federation|Tunisia]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506797
|result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations#Group F|2025 AFCON GS]]
|date       = 31 December
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MOZ}}
|score      = 1–2
|team2      = {{fb|CMR}}
|goals1     = [[Geny Catamo|Catamo]] {{goal|23}}
|goals2     = *[[Nené (footballer, born 1996)|Nené]] {{goal|28|o.g.}}
*[[Christian Kofane|Kofane]] {{goal|55}}
|stadium    = [[Adrar Stadium]]
|location   = [[Agadir]], Morocco
|attendance = 13,093
|referee    = [[Jalal Jayed]] ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506809
|result     = L
}}

===2026===
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025 AFCON R16]]
|date       = 5 January
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|NGA}}
|score      = 4–0
|team2      = {{fb|MOZ}}
|goals1     = *[[Ademola Lookman|Lookman]] {{goal|20}}
*[[Victor Osimhen|Osimhen]] {{goal|25||47}}
*[[Akor Adams|Adams]] {{goal|75}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Fez Stadium]]
|location   = [[Fez, Morocco|Fez]], Morocco
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Abdou Abdel Mefire]] ([[Cameroonian Football Federation|Cameroon]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506815
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| round    = [[Exhibition game#International football|Friendly]]
| date     = 7 June
| time     = {{UTZ|20:00|7}}
| team1    = {{fb-rt|OMA}}
| score    = 4–1
| team2    = {{fb|MOZ}}
| report   =
| goals1   =
*[[Issam Al Sabhi|Al Sabhi]] {{goal|30||51|pen.||59}}
*[[Abdulsalam Al-Shukaili|Al-Shukaili]] {{goal|88}}
| goals2   = *[[Alcides Reis|Reis]] {{goal|62}}
| location = [[Bekasi]], Indonesia
| stadium  = [[Patriot Stadium (Indonesia)|Patriot Stadium]]
| referee  =
| result   = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
| date       = 9 June
| time       = {{UTZ|20:00|7}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|IDN}}
| score      = 1–0
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|MOZ}}
| goals1     = *[[Ole Romeny|Romeny]] {{goal|12}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Gelora Bung Karno Stadium]]
| location   = [[Jakarta]], Indonesia
| attendance = 29,889
| referee    = 
| result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|SEN}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|MOZ}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Senegal
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|MOZ}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|SEN}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Mozambique
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|MOZ}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|SDN}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Mozambique
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|SDN}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|MOZ}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Sudan
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|MOZ}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|ETH}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Mozambique
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|ETH}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|MOZ}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Ethiopia
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}

==Coaching history==
[[File:2008 Portret van Mart Nooij in Heemskerk.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Mart Nooij]] became the manager of Mozambique in 2007]]
{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Cremildo Loforte]] (1980)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Manaca (footballer)|Manaca]] (1986)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[José Geneto]] (1986)
*{{flagicon|RUS}} [[Viktor Bondarenko (football manager)|Viktor Bondarenko]] (1993–1995)
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Rui Caçador]] (1996)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Arnaldo Salvado]] (1996–1998)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Euroflim da Graça]] (1999)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Arnaldo Salvado]] (1999–2000)
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Augusto Matine]] (2001–2002)
*{{flagicon|RUS}} [[Viktor Bondarenko (football manager)|Viktor Bondarenko]] (2003)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Artur Semedo]] (2003–2006)
*{{flagicon|NED}} [[Mart Nooij]] (2007–2011)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Gert Engels]] (2011–2013)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[João Chissano]] (2013–2015)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Hélder Muianga]] (2015)
*{{flagicon|CRO}} [[Boris Pušić]] (2015)
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Abel Xavier]] (2016–2019)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Victor Matine]] (2019)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Luís Gonçalves (football manager)|Luís Gonçalves]] (2019–2021)
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Horácio Gonçalves]] (2021)
*{{flagicon|MOZ}} [[Chiquinho Conde]] (2021–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]] played between 21 December 2025 and 18 January 2026.<ref>{{cite news |title=Conhecido "arsenal" dos Mambas para a final do CAN Marrocos 2025 |url=https://www.fmf.co.mz/news/details/1008 |access-date=18 December 2025 |publisher=Federação Moçambicana de Futebol |date=5 December 2025 |language=Portuguese}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 5 January 2026, after the match against [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Ernan Siluane]]|age={{bda|1998|7|9|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=0|club=[[Associação Black Bulls|Black Bulls]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=GK|name=[[Kimiss Zavala]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|5|8|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[C.S. Marítimo|Marítimo]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=GK|name=[[Ivane Urrubal]]|age={{bda|1997|3|1|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[UD Songo|Songo]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=[[Nanani]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|02|08|df=yes}}|caps=30|goals=0|club=[[UD Songo|Songo]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Nené (footballer, born 1996)|Nené]]|age={{bda|1996|11|15|df=y}}|caps=33|goals=1|club=[[Abu Salim SC|Abu Salim]]|clubnat=LBY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Fernando Chamboco]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|6|15|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Associação Black Bulls|Black Bulls]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=[[Bruno Langa]]|age={{bda|1997|10|31|df=y}}|caps=37|goals=1|club=[[Pafos FC|Pafos]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=DF|name=[[Edmilson Dove]]|age={{bda|1994|7|18|df=y}}|caps=39|goals=0|club=[[Al-Quwa Al-Jawiya]]|clubnat=IRQ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=DF|name=[[Oscar Cherene]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|03|28|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[GDR Textáfrica|Textáfrica]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=DF|name=[[Reinildo Mandava]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|third captain]]|age={{bda|1994|1|21|df=y}}|caps=56|goals=5|club=[[Sunderland A.F.C.|Sunderland]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=DF|name=[[Mexer]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|vice-captain]]|age={{bda|1987|9|8|df=y}}|caps=77|goals=3|club=[[Ankara Keçiörengücü S.K.|Ankara Keçiörengücü]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=DF|name=[[Diogo Calila]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|10|10|df=yes}}|caps=8|goals=1|club=[[C.D. Santa Clara|Santa Clara]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=MF|name=[[Manuel Kambala]]|age={{birth date and age|1991|08|21|df=y}}|caps=42|goals=0|club=[[Polokwane City F.C.|Polokwane City]]|clubnat=RSA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=MF|name=[[Domingues]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]|age={{bda|1983|11|13|df=y}}|caps=124|goals=17|club=[[UD Songo|Songo]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Geny Catamo]]|age={{bda|2001|1|26|df=y}}|caps=43|goals=14|club=[[Sporting CP]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=MF|name=[[João Bonde]]|age={{bda|1997|1|9|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Clube Ferroviário da Beira (football)|Ferroviário Beira]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=MF|name=[[Alfons Amade]]|age={{bda|1999|11|12|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=1|club=[[Dunfermline Athletic F.C.|Dunfermline Athletic]]|clubnat=SCO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Keyns Abdala]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|03|15|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[G.D. Chaves|Chaves]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=MF|name=[[Guima (footballer, born 1995)|Guima]]|age={{bda|1995|11|14|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=2|club=[[Zira FK|Zira]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=28|pos=MF|name=[[Shaquille (footballer)|Shaquille]]|age={{bda|1997|11|24|df=y}}|caps=42|goals=0|club=[[G.D. Sagrada Esperança|Sagrada Esperança]]|clubnat=ANG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Faisal Bangal]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|01|05|df=yes}}|caps=16|goals=4|club=[[AC Mestre|Mestre]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=FW|name=[[Stanley Ratifo]]|age={{bda|1994|12|5|df=y}}|caps=41|goals=9|club=[[BSG Chemie Leipzig (1997)|Chemie Leipzig]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=FW|name=[[Gildo Vilanculos]]|age={{bda|1995|1|31|df=y}}|caps=39|goals=4|club=[[Tadamon SC Sour|Tadamon Sour]]|clubnat=LBN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=FW|name=[[Witi]]|age={{bda|1996|8|26|df=y}}|caps=44|goals=4|club=[[C.D. Nacional|Nacional]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=24|pos=FW|name=[[Melque Alexandre]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|06|26|df=yes}}|caps=27|goals=4|club=[[UD Songo|Songo]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=25|pos=FW|name=[[Chamito]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|01|14|df=yes}}|caps=8|goals=3|club=[[Académico de Viseu FC|Académico de Viseu]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=26|pos=FW|name=[[Ângelo Cantolo]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|05|23|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[CD Chingale|Chingale]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=27|pos=FW|name=[[Clésio]]|age={{bda|1994|10|11|df=y}}|caps=62|goals=9|club=[[Associação Black Bulls|Black Bulls]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for Mozambique in the last 12 months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=14|pos=DF|name=[[Francisco Muchanga|Chico]]|age={{bda|1991|11|5|df=y}}|caps=41|goals=0|club=[[CD Costa do Sol|Costa do Sol]]|clubnat=MOZ|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}, 8 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=14|pos=DF|name=[[Domingos Macandza]]|age={{bda|1998|6|17|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=0|club=[[Associação Black Bulls|Black Bulls]]|clubnat=MOZ|latest=v. {{fb|ALG}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=11|pos=MF|name=[[Pepo Santos|Pepo]]|age={{bda|1994|3|24|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=2|club=[[Caldas S.C.|Caldas]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|CHA}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=6|pos=MF|name=[[Amadú]]|age={{bda|1996|3|12|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=0|club=[[Tanzania Prisons S.C|Tanzania Prisons]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|ALG}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Elias Macamo]]|age={{bda|1994|1|14|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=1|club=[[Clube Ferroviário de Maputo|Ferroviário de Maputo]]|clubnat=MOZ|latest=v. {{fb|ALG}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Kélvio Neves]]|age={{bda|2005|1|2|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Associação Black Bulls|Black Bulls]]|clubnat=MOZ|latest=v. {{fb|ALG}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
''<sup>DEC</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.<br/>''
''<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.<br/>''
''<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br/>''
''<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.<br/>''
''<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team.''
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{See also|Category:Mozambique men's international footballers|label 1=Mozambique men's international footballers}}

{{Updated|5 January 2026}}<ref name="RSSSF">{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Mozambique - Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/moz-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2023-02-02 |archive-date=2022-12-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221209054949/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/moz-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Mozambique.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1.
|align=left|'''[[Domingues|Domingues Pelembé]]'''
|124
|17
|2004–2026
|-
|2.
|align=left|[[Tico-Tico]]
|94
|30
|1992–2010
|-
|3.
|align=left|[[Almiro Lobo|Miró Lobo]]
|84
|8
|2004–2015
|-
|4.
|align=left|'''[[Mexer]]'''
|80
|3
|2007–2026
|-
|5.
|align=left|[[Sérgio Faife]]
|67
|5
|1992–2008
|-
|rowspan=2|6.
|align=left|[[João Rafael Kapango|João Kapango]]
|66
|0
|1996–2012
|-
|align=left|[[Nana Matola]]
|66
|5
|1988–1999
|-
|8.
|align=left|[[Zainadine Júnior]]
|64
|1
|2008–2023
|-
|9.
|align=left|'''[[Clésio]]'''
|63
|9
|2011–present 
|-
|10.
|align=left|[[Momed Hagi]]
|62
|3
|2005–2015
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1.
|align=left|[[Tico-Tico]]
|30
|94
|{{#expr:30/94 round 2}} 
|1992–2010
|-
|2
|align=left|'''[[Domingues|Domingues Pelembé]]'''
|17
|124
|{{#expr:17/124 round 2}}
|2004–2026 
|-
|3.
|align=left|[[Dário Monteiro]]
|16
|48
|{{#expr:16/48 round 2}}
|1996–2011
|-
|4.
|align=left|'''[[Geny Catamo]]'''
|14
|43
|{{#expr:14/43 round 2}}
|2019–present
|-
|5.
|align=left|[[Chiquinho Conde]]
|12
|43
|{{#expr:12/43 round 2}}
|1985–2001
|-
|6.
|align=left|[[Gil Guiamba]]
|11
|22
|{{#expr:11/22 round 2}}
|1980–1983
|-
|7.
|align=left|[[Arnaldo Ouana]]
|10
|37
|{{#expr:10/37 round 2}}
|1987–1999
|-
|rowspan=3|8
|align=left|[[Luís Miquissone]]
|9
|48
|{{#expr:9/48 round 2}}
|2015–2023
|-
|align=left|'''[[Stanley Ratifo]]'''
|9
|50
|{{#expr:9/50 round 2}}
|2017–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Clésio]]'''
|9
|63
|{{#expr:9/63 round 2}}
|2011–present
|}

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|colspan=8 |''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''
|colspan=6 |''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''
|-
| {{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
| colspan=8 |''Not a FIFA member''
| colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| {{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
| colspan=8 |''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 3
| 7
|-
| [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]] and [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
| colspan=8 |''Did not enter''
| colspan=6 |''Declined participation''
|-
| {{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=9 |''Did not qualify''
| 4
| 0
| 1
| 3
| 3
| 11
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
| 2
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 1
| 3
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| 2
| 1
| 0
| 1
| 2
| 2
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 3
| 5
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| 12
| 4
| 3
| 5
| 10
| 10
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 8
| 2
| 3
| 3
| 7
| 11
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 2
| 1
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 1
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|8||3||1||4||5||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10||6||0||4||14||17
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total||–||{{Tooltip|0/11|Number of tournaments qualified for}}||–||–||–||–||–||–
!52
!17
!9
!26
!49
!75
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;font-size:100%;"
!colspan=10|[[African Cup of Nations]] record
!width=1% rowspan=40|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!colspan=10|Appearances: 6
!colspan=6|Appearances: 23
|-
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{Flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 African Nations Cup|1957]]||rowspan=9 colspan="9"|''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''||rowspan=9 colspan="6"|''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''
|-
|{{Flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 African Nations Cup|1959]]
|-
|{{Flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 African Nations Cup|1962]]
|-
|{{Flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 African Nations Cup|1963]]
|-
|{{Flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 African Nations Cup|1965]]
|-
|{{Flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 African Nations Cup|1968]]
|-
|{{Flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 African Nations Cup|1970]]
|-
||{{Flagicon|Cameroon|1961}} [[1972 African Nations Cup|1972]]
|-
|{{Flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 African Nations Cup|1974]]
|-
|{{Flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 African Nations Cup|1976]]||rowspan=2 colspan=9|''Not affiliated to CAF''||rowspan=2 colspan=6|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{Flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 African Nations Cup|1978]]
|-
|{{Flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 African Nations Cup|1980]]||colspan="9"|''Did not enter''||colspan="6"|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{Flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 African Nations Cup|1982]]||rowspan=2 colspan="9"|''Did not qualify''||4||1||1||2||11||8
|-
|{{Flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 African Nations Cup|1984]]||2||1||0||1||3||4
|-
|{{Flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 African Nations Cup|1986]]||Group stage||8th||3||0||0||3||0||7||[[1986 African Cup of Nations squads#Mozambique|Squad]]||6||2||3||1||8||5
|-
|{{Flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 African Nations Cup|1988]]||rowspan=4 colspan="9"|''Did not qualify''||2||0||1||1||3||4
|-
|{{Flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 African Nations Cup|1990]]||2||0||0||2||0||4
|-
|{{Flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Nations Cup|1992]]||4||2||0||2||3||3
|-
|{{Flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 African Nations Cup|1994]]||6||1||3||2||5||7
|-
|{{Flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Nations Cup|1996]]||rowspan=2|Group stage||14th ||3||0||1||2||1||4||[[1996 African Cup of Nations squads#Mozambique|Squad]]||10||6||2||2||16||8
|-
|{{Flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Nations Cup|1998]]||16th||3||0||0||3||1||8||[[1998 African Cup of Nations squads#Mozambique|Squad]]||6||3||1||2||10||7
|-
|{{Flagicon|Ghana}} {{Flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 African Nations Cup|2000]]||rowspan=5 colspan="9" |''Did not qualify''||6||2||1||3||6||10
|-
|{{Flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 African Nations Cup|2002]]||2||1||0||1||1||1
|-
|{{Flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 African Nations Cup|2004]]||6||2||2||2||3||8
|-
|{{Flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 African Nations Cup|2006]]||2||0||0||2||3||5
|-
|{{Flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 African Cup of Nations|2008]]||6||2||3||1||5||4
|-
|{{Flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 African Cup of Nations|2010]]||Group stage||15th||3||0||1||2||2||7||[[2010 Africa Cup of Nations squads#Mozambique|Squad]]||12||4||3||5||10||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]||rowspan=6 colspan="9" |''Did not qualify''||6||2||1||3||4||6
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]||4||1||2||1||4||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]||10||3||5||2||13||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]||6||2||1||3||5||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]||6||2||2||2||7||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]||6||1||1||4||5||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]||Group stage||21st||3||0||2||1||4||7||[[2023 Africa Cup of Nations squads#Mozambique|Squad]]||6||3||1||2||8||9
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]||'''Round of 16'''
|13th
|4
|1
|0
|3
|4
|9
||[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations squads#Mozambique|Squad]]
||6||3||2||1||9||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]||rowspan=2 colspan="9" |''To be determined''||rowspan=2 colspan="6" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!'''Total'''
!'''Round of 16'''
!'''6/35'''
!'''19'''
!'''1'''
!'''4'''
!'''14'''
!'''12'''
!'''42'''
!'''—'''
!
!'''126'''
!'''44'''
!'''35'''
!'''47'''
!'''139'''
!'''145'''
|}

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 2
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]||rowspan=2 colspan="8"|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]||Group stage||16th||3||0||0||3||4||9
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]]||rowspan=3 colspan="8"|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]||Quarter-finals||8th||4||1||1||2||4||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]
| colspan="8" |''Did not qualify''
|-
!'''Total'''
!'''Quarter-finals'''
!'''2/8'''
!'''6'''
!'''1'''
!'''1'''
!'''5'''
!'''8'''
!'''15'''
|}

===COSAFA Cup===
{{main|COSAFA Cup}}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[COSAFA Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (2): [[2008 COSAFA Cup|2008]], [[2015 COSAFA Cup|2015]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (3): [[1997 COSAFA Cup|1997]], [[2009 COSAFA Cup|2009]], [[2024 COSAFA Cup|2024]]

==References==
{{reflist}}

==External links==
{{commons category}}
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20150621034459/http://www.fmf.org.mz/ Federação Moçambicana de Futebol] {{in lang|pt}} (archived 21 June 2015)
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/moz/ Mozambique] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/MOZ Mozambique] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://clubofmozambique.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/Mhoje_mambas7_photo_jpg-800x510.jpg National football team picture]

{{Mozambique national football team}}
{{Navboxes||title=Finalists|list1=
{{2010 Africa Cup of Nations finalists}}
}}
{{Navboxes||title=Squads and managers|list1=
{{Mozambique national football team managers}}
{{navboxes
|title= Mozambique squads
|bg= #D21034
|fg= #FCE100
|bordercolor=black
|list1=
{{Mozambique squad 1996 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Mozambique squad 1998 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Mozambique squad 2010 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Mozambique squad 2023 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Mozambique squad 2025 Africa Cup of Nations}}
}}
}}
{{Football in Mozambique}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Mozambique}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozambique National Football Team}}
[[Category:Mozambique national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
[[Category:National sports teams established in 1975]]
[[Category:1975 establishments in Mozambique]]
